live_path.role: CLI parses --owner and forwards it to the service entrypoint
Orchestrates owner resolution, record creation, routing key derivation, and payload emission
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or defaults
Persists the base record with the resolved owner only
Builds routing_key from the resolved owner and item name
Emits the final payload by merging record fields with derived owner_source and routing_key

logic: If owner is provided and non-blank, return trimmed owner with owner_source=explicit.
If owner is absent or blank, load config/defaults.json owner and return owner_source=default.

routing_key.logic: Lowercase and hyphenate owner and name, then join as '<owner-slug>:<name-slug>'.

emission.note: owner_source and routing_key are appended during serialization after record creation.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner path emits owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
supports: routing_key uses the resolved explicit owner

test_observations.observation: Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and emits owner_source=default.
supports: default resolution happens in service code
storage is not the source of owner_source

test_observations.observation: CLI main forwards --owner into sync_item and the final payload reflects the explicit owner-derived fields.
supports: --owner reaches the service before routing_key emission

test_observations.observation: Docs mention the relevant surfaces but do not prove the support note's inferred storage-layer origin.

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused helper that reads owner_source from a record, but no live path or test calls it.
Legacy helper adds owner_source into a record, but sync_item imports and calls make_record instead.
Legacy routing helper is unused; live code imports build_routing_key from sync_app.serializer.
The note infers storage provenance for owner_source and pre-CLI routing_key computation, which conflicts with the live call chain.
